what_it_does: Declares a confirmed operator-memory rule that forbids unconditional deferrals and requires any parked work to be stored as condition-addressed memory with an explicit return-when trigger.
observations: The file is a data substrate rather than executable logic: it defines a single top-level MEMORY dictionary.
The rule is sourced to a specific decision date (2026-06-10, Decision 4) and a common-memory directive.
It cites the first conditional memory instance: deferred://rg10/static-unverifiable-27.
It explains the rationale: in a 100%-AI-driven system, parked work without a return condition is silently lost.
Status is 'confirmed' with a confirmed date.
standouts: The rule reframes a deferral as a scheduled return tied to an explicit condition.
It references a concrete URI-like deferred-memory token as precedent.
It grounds the rationale in the absence of human re-reading in an AI-driven system.
conventions: Uses a top-level MEMORY dictionary with id, rule, why, evidence, scope, status, and confirmed fields.
Includes a module docstring that restates the rule and references a GC15 row.
Cites evidence as a list of quote/source objects.
concerns: The 'GC15 row' reference in the docstring is not defined inside the file.
The deferred://rg10/static-unverifiable-27 token is mentioned but not explained here.
Scope is a single 'when' clause without examples or validation criteria.
notes: A policy record that turns deferrals into conditional, self-returning memory entries.
questions: What is the exact schema or format for a 'return-when' condition?
How and where is this MEMORY record read or enforced by the operator?
What does 'GC15 row' refer to in this context?
What is the lifecycle of a deferred:// URI once its condition is met?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a no-unconditional-deferrals rule, and the file does exactly that: it documents the rule, rationale, evidence, and scope without enforcement code.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to encode a governance decision as durable, inspectable memory so the operator will not drop parked work and will automatically revisit it when pre-defined conditions become true.
novelty: It treats deferrals not as open-ended todos but as scheduled returns tied to explicit system-memory conditions, with a concrete deferred:// token as precedent.
contribution: It contributes a documented, evidence-backed policy substrate that defines when and how deferrals are permissible, giving a fused system a rule it can consult or enforce for conditional work parking.
summary_for_embedding: A confirmed operator-memory rule stored in a Python module that prohibits unconditional deferrals and requires parked work to be encoded as condition-addressed memory with an explicit return-when trigger.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a governance rule ensuring deferred work is automatically resurfaced by the system rather than forgotten.
